ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Mee Siam. Nearest parent would be a spiced rice noodle dish (FOODON:03315595 — noodle dish). A new FoodOn concept is recommended for this Singapore Malay hawker preparation.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite noodle dishes — rice vermicelli in spiced tamarind broth (closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Mee Siam)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Mee Siam. A03SM used as closest parent for composite rice noodle dishes.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Mee Siam as a specific food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Mee Siam as served at a Singapore hawker centre weighs approximately 360 g and provides around 446 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Mee Siam — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05060085
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code F05060085. This is an exact match for the Singapore hawker preparation of Mee Siam. Minor trace minerals (copper, manganese, vitamin K) supplemented from enriched_json cross-reference at confidence 0.9.

gi_source.title: Rice vermicelli (cooked) — University of Sydney GI Database; composite GI estimated for Mee Siam
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 47 derived from University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked rice vermicelli. Composite GI for Mee Siam estimated at 47 (low), accounting for moderating effects of dietary fibre from vegetables, protein from egg and tofu, and acidity from tamarind. Glycemic load per 360 g serving = (47 × 17.4 × 3.6) / 100 ≈ 29.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 1494 mg per 360 g serving (65% of the 2300 mg daily recommended limit), driven by dried shrimp, belacan paste, and seasoning.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Mee Siam to occasional consumption and request reduced belacan and dried shrimp at hawker stalls to lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (47) rice vermicelli base with moderate glycemic load (~29 per serving); added sugars in tamarind gravy contribute to total carbohydrate load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume Mee Siam in moderate portions (approximately 200–250 g), pairing with additional vegetables to reduce glycemic impact; added sugar in the gravy should be noted and portion size controlled.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g; ~6.5 g per serving) and high sodium (~1494 mg per serving); cholesterol approximately 162 mg per serving from egg and prawns.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit serving frequency due to elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat and cholesterol content; choosing smaller portions and reducing belacan-based gravy can help manage cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat; tamarind provides antioxidants and the spice base may offer mild anti-inflammatory benefits.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Mee Siam is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; the tamarind and chilli base may provide mild antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits, though sodium intake should still be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mee Siam is a signature Singapore and Malaysian Malay hawker dish consisting of thin rice vermicelli (bee hoon) stir-fried or served in a tangy, mildly spicy, sweet-sour gravy made from dried shrimp (hae bee), chilli paste, belacan (fermented shrimp paste), tamarind (asam jawa), and coconut milk. The dish is garnished with halved fried firm tofu (tau pok), hard-boiled egg, bean sprouts, Chinese chives, and prawns, and is finished with a squeeze of fresh calamansi lime and a drizzle of sambal. It exists in two main forms: a dry stir-fried version and a wet version served with a light, flavourful gravy. The flavour profile is a distinctive balance of sour (from tamarind), spicy (from chilli and belacan), and mildly sweet (from added sugar and coconut milk). Mee Siam is considered a uniquely Singaporean-Malay interpretation of Thai-influenced noodle cooking and is commonly found at Malay hawker stalls and food courts. Nutritionally, a standard 360 g plate provides approximately 446 kcal, 17.6 g protein, 14.8 g fat, and 62.6 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 1494 mg per serving, making it a concern for individuals with hypertension. The GI is estimated at 47 (low), with a glycemic load of approximately 29 per serving. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, it is suitable for most adults in moderate portions but requires sodium awareness for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients, and portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo Database under exact food code F05060085, which is the authoritative primary source for Singapore hawker dishes. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database rice vermicelli entry with documented analogous estimation. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are present and supported.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Mee Siam is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo Database (F05060085) as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this composite dish. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as confirmed by HPB source (F05060085). All core macros (protein_g 4.9, fat_g 4.1, carbohydrate_g 17.4) are non-zero and sourced from HPB. Copper (0.08 mg/100g), manganese (0.15 mg/100g), and vitamin K (5.0 mcg/100g) values are from enriched_json cross-reference at confidence 0.9, as these micronutrients are not individually reported in the HPB primary record. GI of 47 is an analogous estimate based on cooked rice vermicelli from the University of Sydney GI Database; composite GI accounts for moderating effects of fibre, protein, and tamarind acidity. Glycemic load per 360 g serving calculated as (47 × 17.4 × 3.6) / 100 = 29.3. The 'set meal' portion (750 g) from the input has been excluded from the default serving as it combines multiple food items and is not representative of Mee Siam alone. Two default portions in the input (360 g and 300 g) have been resolved to 360 g as the HPB-verified standard plate size.
